# Task 2.10 — Audit Trail: `SYSTEM` Actor for Auto-Generated Transactions

> **Roadmap Ref:** Phase 2 — Credit Card & Installment Tracking › Infrastructure
> **Status:** 🔵 `backlog`
> **Last Updated:** 2026-03-12
> **Assignee:** —
> **Estimated Effort:** S

---

## 1. Summary

Ensure that every `transaction` row materialised by the `InvoiceCloser` (Task 2.7) has a corresponding `audit_logs` entry with `actor_id = "SYSTEM"`. This provides a complete, auditable trail distinguishing user-initiated actions from system-generated ones.

---

## 2. Context & Motivation

The Phase 1 audit log records `actor_id` as the authenticated user's ULID. When the `InvoiceCloser` runs automatically (or via API trigger), there is no authenticated user — the actor is the system itself. Introducing the `"SYSTEM"` actor constant and wiring it into the closing flow satisfies the audit requirement without polluting user-context logic.

---

## 3. Scope

### In scope

- [ ] Define `ActorSystem = "SYSTEM"` constant in `internal/domain/audit.go` (or a new `domain/actor.go`).
- [ ] Update `InvoiceCloser.CloseInvoice` (Task 2.7) to pass `ActorSystem` when creating audit log entries for auto-generated transactions.
- [ ] Unit tests verifying that audit logs created by `InvoiceCloser` carry `actor_id = "SYSTEM"`.

### Out of scope

- Changes to Phase 1 audit endpoints or existing audit entries.
- Dashboard/reporting views filtering by actor type (Phase 4+).

---

## 4. Technical Design

### Files to create / modify

| Action | Path                             | Purpose                                               |
| ------ | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| MODIFY | `internal/domain/audit.go`       | Add `ActorSystem` constant                            |
| MODIFY | `internal/service/invoice_closer.go` | Use `domain.ActorSystem` when inserting audit logs |
| MODIFY | `internal/service/invoice_closer_test.go` | Assert `actor_id == "SYSTEM"` in audit mock calls |

### Constant definition

```go
// internal/domain/audit.go

const (
    // ActorSystem is used as actor_id for transactions auto-generated by
    // the InvoiceCloser service — i.e., not triggered by a human user.
    ActorSystem = "SYSTEM"
)
```

### Integration point in `InvoiceCloser`

```go
// Inside CloseInvoice, after inserting the transaction:
err = c.auditRepo.Create(ctx, domain.CreateAuditLogInput{
    TenantID:   tenantID,
    ActorID:    domain.ActorSystem,    // ← SYSTEM, not a user ULID
    Action:     "CREATE",
    EntityType: "transaction",
    EntityID:   newTx.ID,
    Metadata:   /* master_purchase_id, installment_number */,
})
```

### Error cases to handle

| Scenario                               | Handling                                            |
| -------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| Audit log insert fails                 | Rollback entire pgx transaction for this MP; log error |

---

## 5. Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `domain.ActorSystem = "SYSTEM"` constant defined and exported.
- [ ] `InvoiceCloser` uses `domain.ActorSystem` — no hardcoded string literals.
- [ ] Unit tests on `InvoiceCloser` verify mock `auditRepo.Create` is called with `ActorID == domain.ActorSystem`.
- [ ] Audit log `metadata` includes `master_purchase_id` and `installment_number` for traceability.
- [ ] Existing Phase 1 audit log tests are unaffected.
- [ ] `golangci-lint run ./...` passes with zero issues.
- [ ] `gosec ./...` passes with zero issues.
- [ ] `docs/ROADMAP.md` row 2.10 updated to ✅ `done`.

---

## 7. Testing Plan

### Unit tests (`_test.go`, no build tag)

- **File:** `internal/service/invoice_closer_test.go` (extended)
- **Cases:**
  - Happy path: `auditRepo.Create` mock called with `ActorID = "SYSTEM"`.
  - Metadata contains `master_purchase_id` and `installment_number`.
  - Audit insert failure rolls back the pgx transaction.

### Integration tests (`//go:build integration`)

Covered by Task 2.12 — the integration test verifies the audit_logs table contains a `SYSTEM`-actor row after invoice closing.

---

## 8. Open Questions

| # | Question                                                                       | Owner | Resolution |
| - | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----- | ---------- |
| 1 | Should `ActorSystem` be a ULID or a literal string?                            | —     | Literal string `"SYSTEM"` — ULIDs must reference real `users` rows; SYSTEM is not a user. |
| 2 | Should the audit `metadata` be structured JSON or a plain string?              | —     | Structured JSON (map[string]any) — consistent with Phase 1 audit pattern. |
